core_intuitions: Online RL is a TWO-ENGINE LOOP, not a one-engine job: the training data does not exist on disk, it is generated by the model being trained, then scored, then consumed to update that same model — so an inference engine and a training engine run inside one loop on weights that change every step.

There are six invariant components (actor/policy, rollout engine, reward/verifier, learner, reference, experience buffer) plus a seventh controller; frameworks differ only in WHERE these run and WHO coordinates them.

The canonical dataflow is a serial relay: sample -> rollout -> reward -> advantage -> buffer -> learn(E epochs) -> weight-sync; two arrows have NO SFT analogue — the data is manufactured in-loop, and the fresh weights must be synced back into the generator.

Tension 1: generation (memory-bandwidth-bound decode) and training (compute-bound) want OPPOSITE hardware, so their best software (vLLM/SGLang vs FSDP/Megatron) uses INCOMPATIBLE weight layouts — you must run both stacks and bridge them, and that bridge IS the weight-sync problem.

Tension 2: serial stages make the loop a relay race — one GPU pool works while the other sits idle; since generation is 60-80% of the step, a disaggregated training pool can be idle ~80% of the time, which is why colocation and async RL exist.

The whole-part thesis: MEMORY is dominated by the multiplicity of model copies (policy + optimizer state + reference + inference replica + maybe reward/critic, with optimizer state the single biggest block), and TIME is dominated by autoregressive generation.

existing.name: rlanat-six-components
existing.issue: Clean architectural block diagram; controller-at-top, three dataflow boxes, buffer as a cylinder, and the dashed weight-sync arrow read clearly in both light and dark. Relegating actor(2) and reference(6) to bottom annotations (they are weights, not dataflow stages) is a sound choice. Legible, no clipping.

existing.name: rlanat-stepk-dataflow
existing.issue: The seven serial rows (a-g) with the packet animation, the 60-80% callout on rollout, and the emphasized weight-sync feedback arc convey the relay structure well in both themes. Fully understandable as a static frame.

existing.name: rlanat-partvi-roadmap
existing.issue: Vertical spine with three bands (Foundations / Frameworks / Tensions & Components), 'you are here' pin, and faint cross-links back to 6.1 is a clear map of Part VI. Legible in both themes.
existing.improvement: Minor only: the 'FOUNDATIONS' band label sits right at the top edge and nearly touches the 6.1 box corner in the dark render — nudge it down a few px for breathing room. Not blocking.

existing.name: rl-loop
existing.issue: Real overlap and glyph bugs. (1) The '60-80% of step wall-clock' pulse pill sits ON TOP of the 'GENERATION' title in box 1, colliding. (2) Several special glyphs render as missing-glyph boxes: the approx sign before '60-80%', the arrow before 'advantage A-hat', and the arrow near 'rewards R'. (3) In the crowded center, the diagonal 'reads theta' label overlaps the 'co-located on the same GPUs (time-sliced)' caption, and the central ACTOR/POLICY box with its inference/training sub-pills crowds both diagonal labels — the middle is cluttered and letters collide in both themes.
existing.improvement: Move the '60-80%' pill OUT of box 1's title band (place it as a small tab below the box body, or inline in the body text) so it never overlaps 'GENERATION'. Replace every unicode arrow/approx glyph (->, the approx sign) with an SVG marker/path or plain ASCII that the render font supports, so no square boxes appear. Declutter the center: give the ACTOR/POLICY box more vertical clearance from the 'co-located...' caption, and reposition the diagonal 'reads theta' / 'writes theta' labels so they do not cross or touch that caption. Verify in BOTH themes after the fix.

new_figures.name: rlanat-budget-memory-and-time
new_figures.concept: The chapter's whole-part thesis made visual: memory is dominated by the multiplicity of model copies (optimizer state biggest), and one step's wall-clock is dominated by generation.
new_figures.rationale: The chapter states this thesis twice and calls it '80% of RL infrastructure', yet it lives only in prose and two tables. A single side-by-side infographic — a stacked memory bar next to a single-step time bar — is the crystallizing takeaway a reader must carry into all of Part VI; nothing currently visualizes it.
new_figures.placement: A worked example: sizing a 7B GRPO run
new_figures.brief: Two-panel static infographic; clear left-to-right reading order; theme-safe fills only (vary opacity of a neutral + one accent; give the optimizer segment a single distinct hue), small illustrative integers taken from the chapter's own 7B table. LEFT PANEL titled 'MEMORY = how many copies are resident'. Draw ONE vertical stacked bar (GPU memory for a 7B GRPO run), segments stacked bottom->top, each labeled with name + illustrative GB: 'Policy weights 14', 'Policy optimizer (AdamW fp32 master + 2 moments) 84' — draw this segment ~6x taller than the weights and call it out as the single biggest block, 'Policy gradients 14', 'Reference (frozen) 14', 'Inference replica (vLLM) 14' plus a hatched cap labeled 'KV cache (grows with concurrency)'. A right-side bracket totals '~140 GB before activations & KV -> exceeds one 80 GB GPU -> multi-GPU'. Annotate the optimizer block: 'biggest line item -> why critic-free GRPO & LoRA-RL win'. RIGHT PANEL titled 'TIME = generation dominates'. Draw ONE horizontal single-step time bar, segments left->right proportional to seconds: 'Generate ~40 s' (huge, ~78% of the bar), 'Reward ~1 s' (thin sliver), 'Train ~10 s', 'Weight sync ~T_sync' (small hatched, labeled 'varies'). Put each segment's seconds on/under it and mark the generate segment '~78% of the step'. Bottom strap line spanning both panels: 'Memory = model-copy multiplicity. Time = generation. Optimize these two and you understand ~80% of RL infra.' Keep everything inside the viewBox; no overlapping labels; legible in light and dark. Do NOT visualize the idle-pool/colocation story here — that is a separate figure.

new_figures.name: rlanat-relay-idle-timeline
new_figures.concept: Why the serial loop wastes GPU pools (the 'relay race, not a team sport'), and the two escapes that reclaim the idle time: colocation and asynchrony.
new_figures.rationale: Tension 2 is one of the three named structural tensions and has no dedicated figure. Idle time is inherently a TIMELINE/scheduling intuition — which GPU pool is busy WHEN — that prose and the vertical dataflow cannot convey. This figure is the visual setup for a large chunk of Part VI (colocated vs disaggregated, async RL, scaling).
new_figures.placement: Tension 2: idle time — the loop is a relay race, not a team sport
new_figures.brief: A timeline (Gantt-style) infographic, time flowing left->right along a shared x-axis, stacking THREE labeled scenarios vertically. Use a SOLID fill for BUSY blocks and a light striped/hatched fill for IDLE blocks; include a small legend 'busy | idle'. Keep block proportions illustrative (generate ~4x the width of train). SCENARIO A titled 'SYNCHRONOUS DISAGGREGATED (the relay race)': two lanes, 'INFER pool' and 'TRAIN pool'. INFER lane: a wide solid 'generate' block, then a striped IDLE block spanning the train phase. TRAIN lane: a striped IDLE block spanning the generate phase, then a short solid 'train' block, then idle. Annotate the big idle regions ('train pool idle ~80% of the step'; 'infer pool idle during training') and label the scenario 'only one pool works at a time'. SCENARIO B titled 'COLOCATED': a SINGLE merged lane time-sliced 'generate -> train -> generate -> train' back-to-back with no second idle pool; insert a thin gap block labeled 'reshape / free KV <-> optimizer'; annotate 'no idle second pool; pay a local weight+memory reshape'. SCENARIO C titled 'ASYNC': two lanes again, but the 'generate (step k+1)' block on the infer lane OVERLAPS in time with the 'train (step k)' block on the train lane (draw them time-aligned/overlapping); annotate 'generation of step k+1 hidden behind training of step k -> data now slightly off-policy (stale by ~1 step)'. A one-line takeaway under all three: 'Because generation is 60-80% of the step, the fix is to overlap it (colocate/async) so no pool sits idle.' Everything inside the viewBox, no label collisions, legible in both themes. A static frame fully conveys it — idle = visible striped regions; no animation required.

new_figures.name: rlanat-two-profiles-bridge
new_figures.concept: The causal chain behind Tension 1: opposite hardware appetites -> different best software -> incompatible weight layouts -> you must run BOTH stacks and bridge them -> the bridge IS weight sync.
new_figures.rationale: Tension 1 is currently a comparison table plus one line inside the rl-loop actor box. What the table cannot show is the CAUSAL CHAIN that makes weight sync inevitable, anchored by the roofline intuition of two operating points on one chip. This chain is the 'aha' that links Tension 1 to Tension 3; it is foundational but the intuition (not vendor detail) belongs here, with the deep roofline owned by chapter 4.1.
new_figures.placement: Tension 1: generation and training want opposite things
new_figures.brief: A left-to-right directed causal-chain infographic (clean, not cluttered). Start with TWO stacked property cards on the left: 'DECODE (generation)' listing 'memory-bandwidth-bound', 'tiny matmuls, huge KV cache', 'best: vLLM / SGLang', 'weights: paged tensor-parallel layout'; and 'TRAIN (fwd+bwd)' listing 'compute-bound (tensor cores)', 'big fused matmuls + optimizer state', 'best: FSDP / Megatron', 'weights: sharded-optimizer layout'. Between/below the two cards, draw a SMALL roofline thumbnail: one shared little axis pair (x = arithmetic intensity, y = attainable throughput) with the characteristic rising-then-flat roofline curve, and TWO plotted points — 'decode' sitting LOW on the rising (bandwidth-bound) part, 'train' sitting on the FLAT (compute-bound) ceiling — captioned 'same chip, opposite operating points'. Do NOT put any benchmark numbers on the roofline; positions are relative/illustrative only. Then converge both cards with arrows into a middle node 'INCOMPATIBLE weight layouts (you cannot hand vLLM an FSDP shard and serve)', then a final arrow to a highlighted node '=> run BOTH stacks and BRIDGE them', with the punchline label 'the bridge = weight sync (Tension 3)'. Theme-safe colors, everything inside the viewBox, legible in both themes, clear single reading path left->right.

summary: Chapter is already figure-rich (loop, six-components, dataflow, roadmap). Three of the four existing figures are strong keeps; rl-loop needs a refine to fix a title/pill overlap, missing-glyph boxes (arrow/approx chars), and a cluttered center. Two must-have new figures fill the biggest intuition gaps: a memory+time budget infographic that visualizes the part's stated thesis (copies dominate memory, generation dominates time), and a relay-race idle-time timeline for Tension 2 (why colocation/async exist). A third, priority-2 figure draws the Tension-1 causal chain (opposite roofline profiles -> incompatible layouts -> the bridge is weight sync).
